target.title: fix(feishu): drop self-authored receive echoes
target.body:
## Summary

- Drop Feishu receive events authored by the current bot open_id before the in-flight processing claim and inbound debounce queue.
- Also drop receive-shaped app-authored events when the sender app_id matches the monitored Feishu/Lark account app_id, matching the sender shape observed from real Lark bot replies.
- Add focused monitor-message-handler regressions for bot-open-id echoes, current-app echoes, other app-authored senders, and ordinary users.

Fixes #90559.

## Root cause

Feishu `im.message.receive_v1` events enter `createFeishuMessageReceiveHandler`, then immediately claim processing and enqueue into the inbound debouncer. Current `main`, `v2026.6.1`, and `v2026.6.4-alpha.1` have no early self-message filter on that path, so a Feishu/Lark echo of the bot's own send can be treated as fresh inbound input and start another agent turn.

The repair is intentionally bounded to identities that prove the sender is this account: the stored bot open_id, or an app-authored sender whose app_id matches the current account app_id. Other app-authored messages still flow through normal Feishu policy and dispatch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu/Lark bot-send echoes can no longer consume processing/debounce capacity or dispatch another agent turn when the receive event sender open_id matches the configured bot open_id, or when an app-authored receive shape carries the current account app_id.

Real environment tested: Real Lark production app using persistent WebSocket mode, with a real user message in a group where the bot was installed, plus a real bot reply sent through Lark `im.message.reply`. Credentials, tenant, chat, user, message, app, and bot identifiers were redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local Node/tsx proof harness against this checkout, using `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk` persistent connection on the Lark domain; sent one real group message mentioning the bot; let the patched `createFeishuMessageReceiveHandler` process that live event; sent a real bot reply via Lark `im.message.reply`; fetched the reply with `im.message.get`; injected the live-derived current-app sender shape into the patched receive handler to exercise the self-drop boundary.

Evidence after fix:

```text
probe_ok domain=lark appId=cli_...9e17 botOpenId=ou_...c73d
websocket_receive_event senderType=user senderIdTypes=[open_id,union_id,user_id] senderOpenIdMatchesBot=false senderAppIdMatchesApp=false
handler_enqueue enqueueCount=1
handler_dispatch dispatchCount=1
bot_reply_api_result code=0 ok=true
live_bot_reply_sender sender.id=cli_...9e17 sender.id_type=app_id sender.sender_type=app
proof_summary liveUserDispatchCount=1 liveUserEnqueueCount=1 liveBotReplySenderType=app liveBotReplySenderIdType=app_id selfAppDropObserved=true naturalSelfEchoObserved=false
```

Observed result after fix: The real user message from WebSocket reached enqueue and dispatch exactly once. The real bot reply was accepted by Lark and its fetched sender metadata showed `sender_type=app` and `id_type=app_id` for this app. A receive-shaped event derived from that live sender identity was dropped by the patched handler before debounce/dispatch.

What was not tested: This Lark tenant did not naturally emit the bot reply back over `im.message.receive_v1` during the observation window, so the proof does not claim a natural Lark WebSocket self-echo was observed. No China Feishu-domain tenant was available for this credential set. Slow-LLM queue abort, queue-depth backpressure, and session-lock policy remain outside this narrow patch.

## Verification

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/feishu/src/event-types.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.account.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.test.ts && git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-queue.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.webhook-e2e.test.ts` — 5 files, 116 tests passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — final result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported

## Evidence checked

- Feishu/Lark receive-message contract: official Lark docs document `im.message.receive_v1` sender metadata with `sender_id.open_id` and `sender_type`.
- Feishu/Lark message-get contract: official Lark docs and live API output show app/bot-sent messages use message sender metadata with `sender_type=app`; the live reply in this test returned `id_type=app_id`.
- Local dependency contract: `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk@1.66.0` types expose `im.message.receive_v1.sender.sender_id.open_id`, `sender_type`, and message-get sender `id`, `id_type`, `sender_type` fields.
- Current/shipped behavior: `origin/main`, `v2026.6.1`, and `v2026.6.4-alpha.1` route receive events into `tryBeginFeishuMessageProcessing` and `inboundDebouncer.enqueue` without an early self-filter.
target.author: baskduf
